titulo: Decrease Elements To Make Array Zigzag
enunciado:
Given an array 
nums
 of integers, a 
move
 consists of choosing any element and 
decreasing it by 1
.


An array 
A
 is a 
zigzag array
 if either:




Every even-indexed element is greater than adjacent elements, ie. 
A[0] > A[1] < A[2] > A[3] < A[4] > ...


OR, every odd-indexed element is greater than adjacent elements, ie. 
A[0] < A[1] > A[2] < A[3] > A[4] < ...




Return the minimum number of moves to transform the given array 
nums
 into a zigzag array.


 


Example 1:




Input:
 nums = [1,2,3]

Output:
 2

Explanation:
 We can decrease 2 to 0 or 3 to 1.



Example 2:




Input:
 nums = [9,6,1,6,2]

Output:
 4



 


Constraints:




1 <= nums.length <= 1000


1 <= nums[i] <= 1000

hints: Do each case (even indexed is greater, odd indexed is greater) separately. In say the even case, you should decrease each even-indexed element until it is lower than its immediate neighbors.
